bio:
Lindsay Leigh
Recruiter + Working Mom 👩🏼‍💻
I explain how hiring actually works.
descripcion:
#creatorsearchinsights 

One of the fastest ways recruiters can tell whether someone has actually been in leadership?

The way they answer questions about team performance, conflict, or failure.

People who’ve only operated as individual contributors tend to answer from a “what I did” perspective.

Real leaders?
They naturally talk about:
• navigating competing priorities
• coaching underperformance
• protecting team morale
• influencing without control
• making decisions with incomplete information
• taking accountability when outcomes weren’t perfect

Because leadership interviews aren’t really testing whether you can do the work.
They’re testing whether other people can successfully do the work because of you.

And that’s a completely different skill set.

The biggest mistake I see candidates make when interviewing for leadership roles is over-indexing on execution instead of demonstrating judgment, influence, and people management.

If you’re interviewing for management roles, this shift matters more than you think.

Save this for your next interview prep session. 👀

bio:
🥇 Top Voice in Career Advice
✉️ deepali@smoothmedia.co
👇🏽 Your Elite Edge
descripcion:
Your title gave you authority 👑

What you do in your first 90 days determines whether you actually earn power.

Most new leaders confuse the two...

They walk in trying to prove they deserve the role instead of building the trust that lets them actually run it. The instinct to show up with every answer in every meeting feels like competence. It reads to your team as someone who is not listening yet.

The new leaders who actually build power early do the opposite. They ask what is broken. They ask what is political. They ask what real success looks like in six months. Then they shut up long enough to hear the answer 🔑

Your team already knows where the problems are. Your real job is making it safe enough for them to tell you the truth.

Comment ELITE for my free weekly newsletter where I tell you exactly what they will not.

#leadership #newleader #careeradvice #careerstrategy #management

You don’t get the culture you want.

You get the culture you tolerate.

Tolerate gossip?
You’ll get more gossip.

Tolerate negativity?
You’ll get more negativity.

Tolerate mediocrity?
You’ll get more mediocrity.

Every behavior you allow teaches people what’s acceptable.

Culture isn’t built by mission statements.

It’s built by standards.

What you permit, you promote.

bio: Hire Me for Leadership Development Training or Exec Coaching | Author | Speaker | Univ Professor | Jesus Follower | Leadership Training and Speaking Link👇
descripcion:
Most employees don’t need more motivation from leadership.
They need more stability.
Because when teams feel emotionally scattered, performance usually follows right behind it.
The strongest leaders consistently provide three things:
Clarity.
People need to understand what actually matters without constantly interpreting shifting priorities.
Consistency.
Standards, communication, and accountability cannot change based on leadership moods or pressure.
Progress.
People disengage when work starts feeling directionless and growth disappears.
That’s what builds trust over time.
Not inspirational speeches.
Not hype.
Not leadership theater.
I’ve watched teams stay deeply committed through incredibly difficult business seasons because leadership maintained those three things consistently.
People can handle pressure far better than most leaders think.
What wears teams down is chaos, unpredictability, and emotional inconsistency.
Strong leadership is usually more repetitive than people realize:
clear direction,
steady behavior,
forward movement,
repeated long enough that teams begin trusting it. #leadership #leadershipskills #learnontiktok

bio: 💼 Leadership & Ops Mentor | 📜 Storyteller | 🖤Living & Leading from the Heart
descripcion:
The future of leadership is not harder leaders.
Or softer leaders.
It’s more integrated leaders.
Leaders who can:
hold standards without losing humanity
care deeply without over-functioning
communicate directly without becoming cold
stay calm under pressure
think strategically without disconnecting from people
Because leadership is not just execution.
At the next level…
YOU become the strategy.
Try this:
Before every hard decision ask:
What outcome matters?
What standard must remain?
What human impact should I consider?
What response aligns with the leader I want to become?
That’s integrated leadership.
And I think that’s where leadership is going.
Leadership From The Heart
#strategicleadership #leadershipdevelopment #executiveleadership #selfleadership #futureofwork

Helping women land $150K+ roles
Author of Authentic Success
💌 kate@shatteredglasscoaching.com
Book your free call 👇🏼
descripcion:
Perfectly polished communication feels inauthentic.

At the leadership level, many women tend to over-polish their communication. You might rewrite an email seven times until it’s perfect, rehearse a job interview answer until it sounds rehearsed, or get rid of anything that feels too personal or too casual.

People can feel the difference between a real human and a polished script. Polished feels safe. Real feels memorable.

The leaders who have the greatest influence and the strongest connections aren't the most polished in the room. They're the ones who sound most authentically like themselves.

#authenticity #communicationskills #womeninleadership #careercoach #authenticliving

bio: CEO & Founder of Remote Latinos. We hire top talent for businesses 👨🏻‍💻
descripcion:
Never promote someone to manager before they understand this:
Employees get paid to perform. Managers get paid to create performance through others. That's the difference.
The best individual contributor doesn't automatically become the best leader. Because management isn't about:
❌ Doing the work yourself
❌ Being the smartest person in the room
❌ Solving every problem
It's about:
✅ Setting standards
✅ Having difficult conversations
✅ Developing people who can succeed without you
The moment you start rescuing everyone...
You stop leading them.
#leadership
#management
#leadershipskills
#teambuilding
#companyculture

bio:
📚 Systems, leadership & mindset 
for founders + operators scaling
📈 7 Fig COO (7X in 7 months to $1M/mo)
👇 Team meeting playbook for 100% accountability
descripcion:
(psst) Your manager isn't failing to see your potential... your 1:1s just aren't making it visible.
I've run 1:1s from both sides of this table. As a COO reporting to founders, and as a leader managing my own team.
The operators who advance quickly all share the same habit
(let me share it with you now!)
Most leaders show up, give updates, answer questions, and walk out thinking that was productive 🙃
But your manager is forming an opinion of you in every single meeting.
The 1:1 format you use shapes whether they trust you and see you moving up... or not.
👇 4 things to cover on your 1:1s every week:
1️⃣ Start with wins
→ One or two business outcomes you moved since the last check-in.
Not tasks. Outcomes.
(This is how credibility gets built. Quietly, week over week.)
2️⃣ Roadblocks you resolved without them
→ Problems you handled on your own.
This tells them you can carry the authority that comes with a bigger role.
3️⃣ What you need from them
→ Two or three specific asks, framed as "I need X to move Y forward by Z."
Shows you're thinking ahead, not waiting for direction.
4️⃣ Confirm priorities before you leave
→ Your top three for the week, and ask if anything needs to shift.
(They'll feel like they're leading a team that's actually moving together.)
Run this every week.
At some point your manager stops checking in on you and starts counting on you instead.
💻 Comment "NEWSLETTER" if you want to get leadership strategies and frameworks like this delivered to your inbox every week.
👉 Follow @growwithpolina for real-world leadership tools and strategies that actually work
#leadership #managingup #leadershipdevelopment #careergrowth #teammanagement
hashtags: [object Object], leadership, managingup, leadershipdevelopment, careergrowth, teammanagement

bio:
More responsibility. Same title. Still being passed over.
I help high performers stop being overlooked.
Find out what's costing you recognition 👇🏽
descripcion:
Leadership isn’t forming their opinion of you during performance reviews.

They’re forming it in the small moments you think don’t matter.

And a lot of high performers don’t realize how much those moments quietly shape trust, authority, and readiness at work.

1️⃣ Bringing problems to your manager without saying what you think should happen next.
Over time, leadership starts seeing you as someone looking for answers — not someone they trust with harder decisions.

2️⃣ Being the person who always “handles everything.”
You become too valuable where you are — and harder to picture beyond it.

3️⃣ Staying quiet until someone asks for your opinion.
Over time, leadership stops looking to you for direction — and starts looking past you for it.

4️⃣ Constantly saying “we” when talking about results.
Your work becomes visible. Your leadership doesn’t.

5️⃣ Sounding overwhelmed every time pressure hits.
People stop focusing on the problem — and start focusing on whether they trust you under pressure.

None of these things feel big in the moment.

But over time, they quietly shape how leadership experiences your judgment, authority, and readiness.

If this feels a little too familiar — follow along.

This is exactly the kind of workplace dynamic I unpack here.

 

#CareerAdvancement #WomenInLeadership #WorkplaceAdvice #LeadershipDevelopment #promotiontips

descripcion:
Weak leaders create cultures where honest employees eventually stop speaking.

Employees pay attention to how leadership responds to truth.

If honesty creates tension, defensiveness, inconsistency, or political fallout long enough, people stop bringing real problems forward.

That is when organizations begin slowly deteriorating internally.

Healthy companies require leaders who can hear difficult things without becoming emotional, reactive, or insecure.

A culture built on fear eventually creates silence.

And silence inside an organization is expensive.

#leadership #ceo #business #businessowner #jobs

bio:
It’s POSSIBLE!!!

CASHAPP $Shawnston007
descripcion:
Simon Sinek is a renowned author, motivational speaker, and leadership expert best known for his ideas on purpose, inspiration, and human-centered leadership. Born on October 9, 1973, in London and raised in multiple countries, Sinek developed a global perspective that influences his approach to communication and leadership.

He gained worldwide recognition through his influential TED Talk, “Start With Why,” which introduced a simple yet powerful concept: great leaders and organizations succeed because they clearly understand and communicate their purpose. This idea became the foundation of his bestselling book Start With Why, which has inspired millions of people to think differently about leadership, business, and personal growth.

Simon Sinek’s work focuses on building trust, creating meaningful connections, and leading with empathy. He believes that true leadership is not about authority or power, but about responsibility—putting people first and creating environments where individuals feel safe, valued, and motivated to perform at their best. His concept of the “infinite game” emphasizes long-term thinking, resilience, and adaptability rather than short-term wins.

Beyond books and speeches, Sinek continues to influence organizations, entrepreneurs, and individuals around the world. His teachings encourage people to find clarity in their purpose, stay consistent with their values, and inspire others through action rather than words alone.

What makes Simon Sinek especially impactful is his ability to simplify complex ideas into clear, actionable insights. His calm and thoughtful communication style resonates with a wide audience, from business leaders to young professionals seeking direction.

Overall, Simon Sinek represents purpose-driven leadership, authenticity, and long-term thinking. His work reminds people that success is not just about results, but about the impact you create and the people you inspire along the way.

Speaker: Simon Sinek

Follow @value for more entrepreneur stories

bio:

Workplace scripts for real situations
How to lead. Communicate. Hold your ground.
descripcion:
The most dangerous person in any organization is not the bad employee.

It is the leader who refuses to do anything about them.

Every time you protect the wrong person you are punishing the right one. The high achiever carrying the weight. The one delivering every day while the dead weight sits untouched.

They see it. They all see it. And eventually they make a decision.

Quietly. Without drama. And one morning the resignation hits your desk and you act shocked.

You should not be.

Real leadership is not comfortable. If you cannot make the hard call — you are not leading. You are just occupying the chair. 🔥👇

#leadership #leadershipdevelopment #thelegalcoo #worktok #corporatelife

Author of the Leadership Edge | Leadership Coach  | Connect with me
👇🏼👇🏼👇🏼👇🏼
https://linktr.ee/michaelbross or DM me for a consultation
descripcion:
If you’re a leader, here’s another cold hard truth bomb:

Sometimes the best thing you can do is leave.

Not because you’re weak.

Not because you’re giving up.

And not because leadership is hard.

Because you’ve exhausted your ability to create change.

You’ve communicated.

You’ve coached.

You’ve challenged.

You’ve provided solutions.

You’ve fought for the culture.

And nothing changes.

At some point, staying becomes enabling.

Sometimes the most powerful leadership statement you can make is:

👉 “This is not acceptable.”

And then having the courage to walk away.

People often discover the value of leadership after it’s gone.

Sometimes leadership means staying and fighting.

Sometimes leadership means leaving and showing a better way.

🚀 Join our leadership community:

https://www.skool.com/high-performance-3436/about?ref=1ab3db8272904cf4afe2d29dda429ff9

#Leadership  #LeadershipDevelopment #CompanyCulture #SelfLeadership #CareerGrowth

bio:
Mediator | Attorney | Strategist
Turning conflict into clarity & disputes into deals. 🎓 Course Launch: Summer 2026
📍 Miami
@bridgetheconflict
descripcion:
The leaders who scare me most aren’t the ones who get challenged. They’re the ones who never do.Because when people stop disagreeing, stop speaking up, and stop bringing problems to leadership, it doesn’t mean everything is fine. It usually means they’ve learned it’s safer to stay quiet.

The best leaders don’t surround themselves with agreement. They surround themselves with truth. Even when it’s uncomfortable. Especially when it’s uncomfortable.

#Leadership #BridgeTheConflict #ConflictResolution #BusinessLeadership #CommunicationSkills

bio:
Author 📖 "Who'sTalking"
Emotional leadership and self mastery
 🐊 Louisiana 🐊
Work with me 👇🏼
descripcion:
One year of relationship therapy in under five minutes.
Maybe six.

If you’ve ever felt criticized, defensive, disconnected, or stuck in the same argument on repeat, this will explain why.

People only speak from love or a cry for help.
The rest is just fear trying to protect a wound.

If this hit, my book Who’s Talking is available on Amazon and Audible, read by me.
I’m also teaching this live in Dallas March 14–18, 2026.
Details and one on one coaching at thechrishinton.com. #relationshipadvice
#mentalhealthmatters 
#emotionalintelligence
#healingjourney
#selfawareness

bio: ꩜body-led self-growth ꩜1:1 coaching for the woman who craves depth & aliveness
descripcion:
There is so much to consider when making the decision to reconcile with an ex. These are just a few important tips that helped me through the process. There are so many nuances to every couple’s relational dynamics which makes the possibility of reconciling not for everyone. 

After 6 months apart, I had fully moved on and had never given getting back together a second thought but it seemed that God had other plans for me and well, long story short after many long conversations and taking a huge leap of faith here we are.

AND when a man tell’s you that he wants to fight for you and he’s willing to show you through his actions, well the rest is damn history. 

#relationshipdynamics #breakup #relationshipgoals #relationshiptips #relationshipadvice
